results.description: Chromecast was a line of digital media players that allowed users to play online content on a television.

score.why_could_work_now: Chromecast continues to evolve and thrive, so there's no need for a 'resurrection' of a dead product; rather, it is a testament to its successful integration and continuous improvement within Google's ecosystem.

results.description: YouTube Stories (originally YouTube Reels) allowed creators to post temporary videos that would expire after seven days.

score.why_could_work_now: The short-form video market is saturated, and Stories offered limited differentiation and reach compared to established platforms.

results.description: Google Album Archive was a platform that allowed users to access and manage their archived photos and videos from various Google services, such as Hangouts and Picasa Web Albums.

score.why_could_work_now: People still need a reliable, private, and well-integrated platform to store and share their personal photo albums with granular control.

results.description: Google Domains was a domain name registrar operated by Google.

score.why_could_work_now: The demand for straightforward, reliable domain registration with value-added features remains strong among small businesses and individuals.

results.description: Jacquard was a small tag to make it easier and more intuitive for people to interact with technology in their everyday lives, without having to constantly pull out their devices or touch screens.

score.why_could_work_now: The initial product was ahead of its time; recent advancements in conductive thread technology and miniaturization could lead to more practical applications.

results.description: Google Surveys was a business product by Google aimed at facilitating customized market research.

score.why_could_work_now: The need for convenient and cost-effective survey tools still exists, making a similar product viable in today's market.

results.description: Google Code Jam, Kick Start, and Hash Code were competitive programming competitions open to programmers around the world.

score.why_could_work_now: Dedicated competitive programming platforms already exist with large user bases and established ecosystems.

results.description: Google Duo was a video calling app that allowed people to call someone from their contact list.

score.why_could_work_now: The market is saturated with mature, feature-rich video communication apps, and there isn't a compelling new angle Duo could take.

results.description: Android Auto for phone screens was an app that allowed the screen of the phone to be used as an Android Auto interface while driving, intended for vehicles that did not have a compatible screen built in.

score.why_could_work_now: Modern phones have more processing power and better displays, potentially allowing for a more seamless and feature-rich in-car experience without relying on a dedicated head unit.

results.description: Google My Business was an app that allowed businesses to manage their Google Maps Business profiles.

score.why_could_work_now: Small businesses need a dedicated, simple, and powerful app to manage their online presence, reviews, and customer interactions directly, without being buried in the general Google Maps app.

results.description: YouTube Go was an app aimed at making YouTube easier to access on mobile devices in emerging markets through special features like downloading video on wifi for viewing later.

score.why_could_work_now: A lightweight YouTube Go could thrive as a default app on entry-level Android devices given growing smartphone penetration in emerging markets.

results.description: Google Contributor was a program run by Google that allowed users in the Google Network of content sites to view the websites without any advertisements that are administered, sorted, and maintained by Google.

score.why_could_work_now: Increased desire for ad-free experiences and creator support combined with a more mature micropayment infrastructure.

results.description: Google Maps Coordinate was a service for managing mobile workforces with the help of mobile apps and a web-based dashboard.

score.why_could_work_now: Modern communication tools and more sophisticated mapping APIs could allow for a more integrated and user-friendly experience for location coordination.

results.description: Fitbit Coach (formerly Fitstar) was a video-based bodyweight workout app that used AI to personalize workouts based on user feedback.

score.why_could_work_now: People are increasingly focused on personalized wellness and are comfortable with digital coaching, especially when combined with wearable data.

results.description: Fitstar Yoga was a video-based yoga app that created unique yoga sessions based on user preference and skill level.

score.why_could_work_now: A modern, AI-driven yoga app could offer personalized experiences and real-time feedback, addressing user desire for customized wellness.

results.description: Analytics platform for Google's Dialogflow chatbot & others, started by the Google-funded Area120 incubator then retired and partially merged into Dialogflow itself.

score.why_could_work_now: The demand for easy-to-implement, customizable chatbot analytics has only grown with the proliferation of AI tools and conversational interfaces, especially if it integrates seamlessly with diverse platforms beyond just Dialogflow.
